evidence-binding: n3-adversarial b3c5c502f2d0
verdict: pass

RE-VERIFICATION after the R1 repair (auto-takeover removed; fail-closed typed stale refusal). Round-1 refutation (concurrent stale-takeover double-acquire, 6/500 at N=2 and 111/500 at N=6 on the real function) is STRUCTURALLY ELIMINATED: the only creation path is openSync 'wx' (schema:781); on EEXIST the code only classifies {ok:false, stale, holder}; the only unlink sites are the holder's own release (main() finally, adaptive-node:5544) and the own-lock exit hook; repo-wide grep shows no other scheduler.lock toucher; no code path from a stale VERDICT to an unlink.

Empirical re-attack (same harness that broke round 1): N=6 x 500 trials -> 0 acquisitions (was 111/500); N=2 x 500 -> 0 (was 6/500); fresh-lock contention with holding children N=6 x 60 -> exactly one holder per trial, 0 interval overlaps (round-1 "81 multi-acquire" reading was a harness artifact — sequential exit-hook release).

Classification worst case is a wrong HINT, never a wrong acquire (matrix verified: dead-pid->stale:true, live->false, empty+fresh mtime->false protecting a mid-write holder, cross-host by age). No wedge: scheduler_lock_stale hint names pid/host/since + exact rm "<lockPath>" one-session recovery; recovery narrative test pins hinted-unlink -> re-run -> opens. Coverage complete: all 14 writeFile(planPath) sites + all running-set mutators map to guarded subcommands; record-evidence writes only .cache evidence + O_APPEND jsonl telemetry — safe unlocked. Serial regression: none (1127 assertions exit 0; walkthrough pass; validate-script-sync ok; schema md5-identical x4). Test honesty: T-585-stale-race deterministically catches a reintroduced takeover (first taker always acquires under takeover) and the byte-identical-lockfile assertion catches any non-holder unlink even absent an acquire. #590 reorder intact and crash-safe post-repair.

Residual risks (non-blocking, design-accepted): PID reuse -> wrong-flavor refusal (scheduler_locked instead of stale) until the recycled-pid process exits, manual rm recovers; create-then-write-failure orphan -> up to LANE_STALENESS_MS wrong-flavor refusals, mtime fallback then flips stale, manual rm recovers; operator-misuse cross-unlink inherent to manually-recoverable lockfiles (mitigated by ONE-session hint wording); shared-FS O_EXCL NFSv2 caveat outside the single-host model; freeze-time plan writes (handoff/--freeze) are planner-stage, outside the two in-scope scheduler races.

History: round 1 = verdict fail (R1 in_run_repair, severity high); repaired via architect re-decision (remove takeover, AC's typed-operator-hint disjunct) + reopen-node n2-impl; this re-verification passes.
